items.summary: A consortium headed by Adroit Capital has been announced as the preferred bidder for the smelter in northern Tasmania, as federal and state government pledge another $5 million to support workers in the weeks ahead.

items.title: Scholarship to be created in memory of farmer Callum Smail after death at 26
items.summary: A scholarship will be created at a Victorian agricultural college in memory of a young man who died in a farming accident last month.

items.title: Alexia Putellas ends 14-season Barcelona career after fourth Champions League title
items.summary: Alexia Putellas ends her stint at Barcelona after winning 10 league titles and four Champions Leagues with the Spanish giants.

items.title: Australia's Kimberly Birrell stuns Jessica Pegula as Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka dazzle at French Open
items.summary: Australia's Kimberly Birrell continues a day of upsets for Australia by advancing to the second round of the French Open for the first time in her career.

items.title: Government pitches largest overhaul to unemployment system in decades
items.summary: Jobseeker services will shift away from a one-size-fits-all approach to a new tiered model, to be unveiled by the government later today.

items.title: St John Ambulance NT considering body-worn cameras amid ongoing assaults on paramedics
items.summary: The Northern Territory's ambulance service is considering introducing police-style body-worn cameras after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis led to rioting in Alice Springs last month.

items.title: BP removes chair Albert Manifold over 'serious conduct and governance concerns'
items.summary: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and "overbearing" behaviour, which he later described as a "false narrative".

items.title: NSW coach Laurie Daley says Nathan Cleary unfairly judged over losing State of Origin record
items.summary: NSW coach Laurie Daley says the responsibility does not solely rest on the shoulders of his star No.7 ahead of this year's State of Origin campaign.

items.title: Brad Scott reveals he wanted Essendon to trade Zach Merrett, confirms bumper Harley Reid offer
items.summary: The ousted Essendon coach reveals he was against the decision to keep Zach Merrett at the club despite a late trade offer from Hawthorn.

items.title: Australian Adam Walton stuns former world number one Daniil Medvedev at French Open
items.summary: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

items.title: Peer support worker Brandie McGrady is a Dubbo Drug Court success story
items.summary: The peer support worker at Dubbo's Bunmabunmarra service knows firsthand how the drug court can offer a second chance.

items.title: M Resources and Jindal Steel announced as two remaining Whyalla steelworks bidders
items.summary: Peter Malinauskas has announced M Resources and Jindal Steel are the two bidders remaining for the financially stricken Whyalla steelworks, while BlueScope Steel retains its right of last offer.

items.title: State of Origin star Cameron Munster's Maroons triumph anniversary brings heartache
items.summary: Cameron Munster's mum reveals the heartbreaking call she had to make before her superstar son led Queensland to victory days after the shock death of his dad.

items.title: Iran calls latest US strikes 'bad faith' sign as internet is slowly restored
items.summary: Iran has denounced US strikes in the country's south as a sign of "bad faith and unreliability" as negotiations toward a possible deal to end the war continue.

items.title: ACT to unify public school system in 'generational shift' amid significant funding pressure
items.summary: The ACT's public education system will be significantly reformed after a review of resourcing found schools operated much like "independent small businesses".

items.summary: Rare baby turtles that are a cross between the loggerhead and green species have been found on the beach at Mon Repos.

items.title: Wiradjuri community devastated by destruction of rock shelter by ACEREZ
items.summary: Members of a First Nations community in Central West NSW say they were devastated to learn a culturally significant rock shelter had been destroyed to make way for a renewable energy project.

items.title: Fidel Castro's daughter fled his regime. Now, she hopes the US can bring it down
items.summary: Fidel Castro's daughter says the US has failed to find a way to deal with Cuba for decades. But she tells the ABC that the Trump administration has a difference that could spell the end of the regime she fled.

items.title: Hungry eleven-armed seastars making a meal of Tasmanian scallops
items.summary: The fast spread of pest eleven-armed seastars has fishers, along with researchers, concerned.

items.title: How did almost 90 drones crash during Sydney's Vivid festival?
items.summary: The company behind the drone shows at Sydney's Vivid festival said spectators were not at risk when almost 90 devices fell from the sky during a display on Monday night.

items.title: Women and children linked to Islamic State spend first night on Australian soil
items.summary: A group of so-called ISIS brides who left the notorious al-Roj camp in Syria last week have spent their first night home in Australia.

items.title: BHP is rightly sensitive about its reputation as a corporate leader on climate change
items.summary: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

items.title: Finding out you are adopted or donor-conceived after taking an ancestry DNA test
items.summary: Direct-to-consumer genetic testing kits are often given as gifts or purchased "as a bit of fun", which means people often aren't prepared to discover unexpected parents, siblings and other relatives.

items.title: Brad Scott sacking a chance for Essendon to step out of James Hird's shadow

items.title: Wallabies facing same selection headache as State of Origin's NSW Blues

items.title: School students have grown up online but test shows digital literacy at new low
items.summary: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.

items.title: Iran war derailed talks to claw back billions in tax credits paid to miners

items.summary: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

items.title: New Blak Douglas work to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art

items.summary: The British capital's previous hottest May day was set only one day prior, when the mercury topped 33.5C.

items.title: Ricky Hampson discovered he was a Stolen Generations survivor. Now, he's calling for action
items.summary: Ricky Hampson spent most of his life believing he had been adopted before learning several years ago he is a survivor of the Stolen Generations. Today, he is calling for more action and truth-telling.

items.title: Iluwanti Ken highlights mistreatment of APY Art Centre Collective after 'white hands on black art'
items.summary: Award-winning artist Iluwanti Ken says the withdrawal of funding and support over the "white hands on black art" scandal has left her feeling like a stranger in her own industry.

items.title: Navy chief says Chinese flotilla in Tasman Sea stirred anxiety and anger in Australia, NZ
items.summary: Australia's Chief of Navy says China stirred "anger" in New Zealand when it sent a powerful naval flotilla into the Tasman Sea last year, encouraging political support on both sides of the ditch for increased defence spending.

items.title: Another group of women and children linked to Islamic State group return to Australia
items.summary: Two flights carrying women and children linked to the Islamic State group have landed in Australia.

items.title: Shoko Kawata to become first mayor in Japan to take maternity leave while in office
items.summary: A Japanese mayor will take maternity leave this year, becoming the first in the country's history to do so while in office.

items.title: With 'advantage' shifting to Ukraine, Russia hits Kyiv with Oreshnik hypersonic missile
items.summary: Russia escalates attacks on Kyiv by using a hypersonic missile in one of its largest assaults on the Ukrainian capital since the start of the four-year war, and warns foreigners to leave the city ahead of further strikes.

items.title: BHP dumped plans for processing plant that would cut global emissions by 1.7 million tonnes a year
items.summary: Internal BHP documents reveal how the mining giant planned to set up an iron ore processing facility in the Pilbara to cut carbon emissions before backing away from it entirely.

items.title: Kumanjayi White family 'heartbroken' no charges laid over custody death
items.summary: The family of Kumanjayi White tell the ABC they are "heartbroken" that no charges will be laid over his death in custody, as Northern Territory Police confirm their criminal investigation has concluded.

items.title: What are Trump's Abraham Accords, and will other countries join?
items.summary: As US President Donald Trump pushes for a deal with Iran, he is reviving one of the signature foreign policy projects of his first term: the Abraham Accords.

items.title: Brinkworth family loses appeal over judgement that father was responsible for Blackford bushfire
items.summary: A court judgement that found pastoralist Tom Brinkworth liable for a 2021 bushfire, five months after he died, has been upheld after an appeal by Mr Brinkworth's family.

items.title: Sonny Rollins, saxophonist and restless genius of jazz, dies at 95
items.summary: Rollins, one of the greatest saxophonists of all time, played with everyone from Miles Davis to The Rolling Stones.

items.title: Why rugby league needs State of Origin to save a lost NRL season
items.summary: Players, coaches and fans are disillusioned with the rule changes that have come to define this NRL season but ahead of Origin I can the interstate series put a broken season back together?

items.title: When moving forward means being the cycle-breaker in a family

items.title: NT diphtheria cases falling as 10,000 vaccines rolled out, test confirms man's death related
items.summary: The Northern Territory government says the rate of new diphtheria cases in the territory has fallen from more than 20 per week to less than 10 after 10,000 Territorians stepped up to be immunised against the ongoing outbreak.

items.title: Royal commissioner issues warning over harassment of witnesses, officer defends patrols at Bondi Hanukkah event
items.summary: Four officers were present at the event when the shooting started, with Jewish security organisation Community Security Group NSW denied its request to have police on patrol for the entirety of the event.

items.title: Federal department says Tasmania failed to deliver 'sensible or useable' Nuyina icebreaker refuelling solution
items.summary: A federal department says the Tasmanian government's proposed interim solution to a costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker is "far from" acceptable.

items.title: Government sets up fight with second-largest gas exporter Santos over domestic reservation plan
items.summary: The federal government's draft framework would require gas producers to supply 20 per cent of their exports to the domestic market, with limited exceptions, which gas producers warn would "undermine" future supply.

items.title: Grain growers adapt as low May rainfall highlights a drying autumn trend
items.summary: A downward trend in autumn rainfall has WA farmers adapting to early seeding, but hoping this week's forecast cold front will provide a much-needed break to the dry spell.

items.summary: A "tragedy of the trolleys" is unfolding in Rockhampton, where a lack of governance over waterways is creating an environmental hazard.

items.summary: One of Israel's most prominent opposition politicians has called for an investigation into Israel's far-right national security minister, after he posted video showing alleged abuse of activists from the Global Sumud Flotilla last week.

items.title: 'Morale' boost for Brokenwood Wines after 2026 NSW President's Medal win
items.summary: A winery squeezing value out of every part of the vine has received the country's top agricultural gong at a difficult time for the wine industry.

items.title: Riley McGree to miss FIFA World Cup for Socceroos with injury
items.summary: The Socceroos have been dealt a massive injury blow ahead of next month's FIFA World Cup, with midfielder Riley McGree ruled out of the tournament.

items.title: 'Inhumane' NDIS home in Broome subject to allegations of neglect
items.summary: Former workers at a disability home in Western Australia's far north raise allegations of neglect, including medical mismanagement, locked fridges and a child being placed at risk.

items.title: A century on from Miles Davis's birth, his legacy still shapes jazz
items.summary: From Illinois to New York to Australia, legendary trumpeter Miles Davis helped shape the direction of jazz with albums like Birth of the Cool and Kind of Blue.

items.title: Essendon part ways with Brad Scott as AFL head coach

items.title: Vivid Sydney drone shows cancelled for now after malfunction saw 89 drones fall into the harbour
items.summary: The incident occurred not long into Monday's 7:30pm "star-bound" show when almost 90 drones began falling out of formation and into the harbour around Cockle Bay.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Teen star Eva Carneiro becomes Super Netball's youngest-ever player
items.summary: The Giants team is flailing at the bottom of the ladder, winless after 11 rounds and the likely recipient of this year's wooden spoon. But their brand new debutant Eva Carneiro has brought some much needed excitement for fans, as the teenager breaks the record for the youngest-ever player in Super Netball.

items.title: Bringing Them Home report inaction a 'failure', former commissioner says
items.summary: Mick Dodson heard "soul-destroying" testimony from more than 500 First Nations people during a landmark inquiry. He says that 29 years later, it's time for action.
